Risk Description: Because of only 34% project completion against expected 100% completion as of Feb 25, 2026, and pending approval of revised project plan from AXOS, there is a risk that the Phase 2 delivery timeline will be significantly exceeded, which could lead to contractual penalties, budget overruns due to extended resource engagement, client dissatisfaction, and potential contract termination.
Potential Impact: Major schedule slippage beyond contracted dates may trigger penalty clauses in the SOW. Extended timeline increases resource costs and reduces profit margins. Client relationship strain could impact future business. Potential for scope reduction or contract renegotiation. Reputational damage in the finance/banking sector.

SLA Metric: Defect Density: 22.19 bugs/KLOC (Target: 2 bugs/KLOC); Defect Leakage: 4.8% (Threshold: ≤5%)
Risk Title: Critical Quality and Defect Management Risk
Risk Description: Because of defect density at 22.19 bugs per KLOC (11x higher than the 2 bugs/KLOC target) and defect leakage at 4.8% (approaching the 5% threshold), with 44 total bugs as of Feb 24, 2026, there is a risk that product quality will be severely compromised, which could lead to failed UAT, production defects post-deployment, extensive rework costs, delayed go-live, SLA breaches, and loss of client confidence in Ascendion's delivery capability.
Tech Stack: QA Testing, JIRA, API Testing, UI Testing
Potential Impact: High probability of UAT rejection requiring extensive rework. Production deployment risks with potential system failures or data integrity issues. Increased support and maintenance costs post-go-live. Client may invoke warranty clauses or demand credits. Damage to Ascendion's reputation for quality delivery. Potential for independent quality audits and additional governance overhead.

Timeline: Bank Accounts: 3/2, Vendors: 3/9, Delegations: 3/20, Transactional Images: 3/20
SLA Metric: Multiple module UAT dates delayed and paused
Risk Title: Module Delivery Dependency and Sequencing Risk
Risk Description: Because of multiple module UAT delays (Bank Accounts paused until 3/2, Vendors until 3/9, Delegations until 3/20, Transactional Images until 3/20) and dependencies between modules (Deposits/Receipts/Commissions must be tested together), there is a risk that downstream module testing and integration will be blocked, which could lead to cascading delays across all Release 1 deliverables, compressed testing windows, inadequate integration testing, and potential for critical defects in production.
Potential Impact: Cascading delays across dependent modules creating a domino effect. Compressed UAT windows may force inadequate testing coverage. Integration issues between modules may only surface late in the cycle. Risk of partial go-live or phased rollout adding complexity. Resource allocation conflicts as multiple modules compete for testing environments and personnel. Client may lose confidence in delivery capability and escalate concerns to executive leadership.

SLA Metric: NACHA integration pending due to AXOS APIs not available
Risk Description: Because of pending AXOS APIs required for NACHA integration in banking services, and dependencies on Axos Bank for banking product integration (per SOW Section A.c), with only Entitlement and BAI2 automation tests completed successfully, there is a risk that core banking functionality cannot be completed or tested, which could lead to incomplete Release 1 scope, inability to process critical financial transactions, failed compliance requirements, and potential contractual disputes over deliverable acceptance.
Tech Stack: Banking APIs, NACHA, BAI2, Entitlement Services, Cloud Integration
Potential Impact: Core banking functionality blocked until AXOS delivers required APIs. Cannot validate end-to-end transaction flows without NACHA integration. Compliance and regulatory risks if payment processing capabilities are incomplete. May require scope reduction or deferral to future releases. Client relationship strain due to dependency on their internal teams. Potential for finger-pointing and contractual disputes over responsibility for delays. Financial impact if banking integration is a go-live gate condition.

SLA Metric: Environment downtime impacting enterprise services in DEV/QA
Risk Title: Infrastructure Stability and Environment Availability Risk
Risk Description: Because of recurring network issues causing environment downtime in DEV/QA environments (tracked since Jan 19, 2026), Burpsuite integration issues blocking QA deployment, and email notification service down in DEV due to Salesforce issues, there is a risk that development and testing activities will be frequently interrupted, which could lead to lost productivity, extended testing cycles, inability to meet sprint commitments, and accumulated technical debt from workarounds.
Tech Stack: AWS, Azure, Burpsuite, Salesforce, DevOps, CI/CD
Potential Impact: Frequent interruptions reduce team productivity and morale. Testing cycles extended due to environment unavailability. Developers may implement workarounds that create technical debt. Security testing blocked by Burpsuite issues creates compliance gaps. Difficult to maintain sprint velocity and predictability. May require escalation to AXOS executive leadership. Could necessitate alternative infrastructure solutions or redundancy.

SLA Metric: Reporting scope to be revisited and replanned based on POC reviews
Risk Title: Reporting Scope Ambiguity and Rework Risk
Risk Description: Because of ongoing POC reviews for reporting functionality and pending scope prioritization discussions, with Milestone 1 scope including only 7 standard reports with SAVE AS capability and module reports (paginated reports), and Billie 2 UI screen added back into scope requiring new estimation, there is a risk that reporting requirements will undergo significant changes, which could lead to rework, scope creep, estimation inaccuracies, and delays in Milestone 1 delivery.
Tech Stack: Power BI, Angular, Reporting Services, Paginated Reports
Potential Impact: Scope changes require re-estimation and re-planning impacting schedule. Rework on completed reporting components wastes effort and budget. Unclear requirements lead to multiple development iterations. Client expectations may not align with technical feasibility. Integration complexity with Power BI may be underestimated. May require additional licensing costs for reporting tools. Could impact downstream milestones if reporting is a dependency.

SLA Metric: UI: ≤10 sec, API: ≤3 sec (Performance SLAs not yet tested)
Risk Title: Performance Testing Readiness and SLA Compliance Risk
Risk Description: Because of pending performance testing strategy discussions (scheduled for 3/3-3/5), with stringent performance SLAs of ≤10 seconds for UI page rendering and ≤3 seconds for API response time not yet validated, and uncertainty around test environment, synthetic data, and Loadrunner tool usage, there is a risk that the system will fail to meet performance requirements, which could lead to failed UAT, production performance issues, SLA breaches, user dissatisfaction, and potential need for architectural refactoring.
Tech Stack: Loadrunner, Performance Testing, UI (Angular), API (.NET), SQL Server
Potential Impact: Performance failures could block UAT acceptance and go-live. Architectural changes may be needed if performance issues are fundamental. Late discovery of performance issues extends timeline significantly. May require infrastructure upgrades (cost impact). User experience degradation leads to adoption resistance. SLA breaches could trigger contractual penalties. Reputation damage if production performance is poor.

SLA Metric: UI Test Automation: 68%; API Test Automation: 22%
Risk Description: Because of API test automation at only 22% completion (target: 50%) with 300+ APIs to automate, and ongoing investigation into using agents for API regression automation (ETA 3/16), there is a risk that regression testing will be manual, time-consuming, and error-prone, which could lead to extended testing cycles, missed defects in regression, inability to support continuous integration, and increased testing costs.
Tech Stack: Postman, API Testing, Test Automation, AAVA Agents
Potential Impact: Manual regression testing is time-consuming and delays releases. Increased risk of regression defects slipping into production. Cannot support rapid iteration or continuous deployment. Higher testing costs due to manual effort. Difficult to maintain test coverage as API count grows. Agent-based automation may not be feasible requiring alternative approaches. Testing becomes a bottleneck for delivery velocity.

Financial Metric: Cost approval secured for AWS Bedrock Claude Sonnet 4.5
Risk Title: AI Infrastructure and Service Enablement Risk
Risk Description: Because of the need for AXOS infrastructure team to enable Bedrock services in AWS for Billie Part 2 (AI invoice processing), and dependency on AWS Bedrock Claude Sonnet 4.5 LLM which requires CISO and cost approvals (now secured), there is a risk that infrastructure enablement delays will block Billie Part 2 completion, which could lead to Milestone 1 delivery delays, inability to demonstrate AI capabilities to client, and potential scope deferral.
Tech Stack: AWS Bedrock, Claude Sonnet 4.5, LLM, AI/ML, OCR
Potential Impact: Billie Part 2 blocked until AWS Bedrock is enabled in environment. AI capabilities cannot be demonstrated or tested without infrastructure. Client expectations for AI-driven invoice processing may not be met. May require alternative AI solutions if AWS Bedrock enablement is delayed. Cost and CISO approval process could be repeated if changes are needed. Milestone 1 delivery at risk if Billie Part 2 is a critical component.

Financial Metric: Go Green Plan: +15 testers, +15 developers, +3 onshore +1 offshore data modelers
Risk Title: Resource Capacity and Skill Gap Risk
Risk Description: Because of identified need for significant additional resources (15 testers, 15 developers, 4 data modelers) as part of the Go Green Plan to address project delays, and challenges in data modeling requiring Erwin tool and dedicated data modeling team, there is a risk that resource onboarding will be delayed or inadequate, which could lead to continued velocity issues, quality problems due to insufficient expertise, knowledge transfer gaps, and inability to recover project schedule.
Tech Stack: Erwin, Data Modeling, QA, Development, .NET, Angular
Potential Impact: Delayed resource onboarding extends recovery timeline. New resources require ramp-up time reducing immediate productivity. Knowledge transfer burden on existing team members. Risk of hiring resources with inadequate skills for complex finance domain. Increased costs for additional resources impact project margins. May not be able to find qualified resources in required timeframe. Team coordination and communication overhead increases with larger team.

SLA Metric: USPS address validation, RBAC, File upload, Scheduler, Notifications pending
Risk Title: Shared Services Delivery and Reusability Risk
Risk Description: Because of shared services components (USPS address validation, RBAC, File upload, Scheduler, Notifications) still in refinement and grooming phase with development not yet started, and these services being dependencies for multiple modules across Milestones 2 and 3, there is a risk that module development will be blocked or delayed, which could lead to cascading delays across multiple milestones, duplicated effort if modules build their own solutions, and inconsistent implementation patterns.
Tech Stack: USPS API, RBAC, File Management, Scheduler, Notification Services
Potential Impact: Multiple modules blocked waiting for shared services. Risk of duplicated effort if modules implement their own solutions. Inconsistent patterns across modules if shared services not used. Difficult to maintain and enhance if not centralized. May require architectural rework if shared services approach is abandoned. Testing complexity increases with dependencies. Integration issues may surface late when modules try to consume shared services.

SLA Metric: Data migration strategy and estimates not yet known
Risk Title: Data Migration Strategy and Execution Risk
Risk Description: Because of data migration strategy not yet finalized with volume of work and ability to have 5 beta customers ready at go-live unknown, and engagement of data practice team to leverage expertise still in progress, there is a risk that legacy Zenith data cannot be migrated to NexLedgr in time for go-live, which could lead to delayed customer onboarding, manual data entry requirements, data integrity issues, and inability to achieve production cutover.
Tech Stack: Python, C#, Data Migration, SQL Server, Legacy Zenith System
Potential Impact: Go-live blocked if customer data cannot be migrated. Manual data entry is time-consuming, error-prone, and not scalable. Data integrity issues could cause financial discrepancies. Customer onboarding delays impact revenue realization. May require phased migration approach adding complexity. Legacy system dependencies may be discovered late. Compliance and audit requirements for data migration may be complex.

SLA Metric: Security vulnerabilities: TBD (Internal review in progress)
Risk Description: Because of security vulnerabilities status listed as 'TBD' with internal BurpSuite review in progress, and BurpSuite integration issues blocking QA deployment, with results needing to be acted upon, there is a risk that critical security vulnerabilities exist in the application, which could lead to failed security audits, compliance violations, data breaches, regulatory penalties, and inability to deploy to production.
Tech Stack: BurpSuite, Security Testing, Authentication, Authorization, RBAC
Potential Impact: Security vulnerabilities could block production deployment. Compliance violations may result in regulatory penalties. Data breach risk exposes customer financial data. Reputational damage and loss of customer trust. May require architectural security changes if fundamental issues found. Client may mandate independent security audit. Could trigger SOW penalty clauses for security non-compliance.

SLA Metric: Rollfi payroll integration: 19 UI/UX screens, 59 APIs
Risk Description: Because of dependency on third-party provider Rollfi for payroll functionality (19 UI/UX screens, 59 APIs) as specified in SOW Section A.b, with Zenith responsible for facilitating Rollfi's cooperation and any change in third-party provider requiring change management process, there is a risk that Rollfi integration will face delays, API limitations, or functional gaps, which could lead to incomplete payroll functionality, scope disputes over functional parity with Current Zenith System, and potential need to change providers mid-project.
Tech Stack: Rollfi APIs, Payroll Integration, UI/UX, Third-Party Services
Potential Impact: Rollfi API limitations may prevent functional parity with legacy system. Integration delays impact payroll module delivery. Scope disputes over what constitutes functional parity. Changing providers mid-project requires significant rework. Client relationship strain if payroll functionality is incomplete. Compliance risks if payroll processing is incorrect. May require contract amendments and change orders.

SLA Metric: Public/Private API separation, DNS configuration, deployment pipelines
Risk Title: CI/CD Pipeline and API Architecture Risk
Risk Description: Because of pending implementation of public and private API separation with distinctive DNS mappings, and need to enforce service account token authentication for all private APIs, with coordination required with QA for authorization scenarios and approval component split, there is a risk that deployment architecture will be incomplete or incorrectly configured, which could lead to security vulnerabilities, deployment failures, inability to support production operations, and potential for unauthorized API access.
Tech Stack: CI/CD, Azure DevOps, DNS, API Gateway, Authentication, Authorization
Potential Impact: Security vulnerabilities if API separation not properly implemented. Deployment failures could cause production outages. Difficult to troubleshoot issues without proper API separation. May require architectural rework if design is flawed. QA testing complexity increases with public/private API scenarios. Production support challenges if API routing is misconfigured. Could impact performance if API gateway not properly designed.
